specification.topic-statement: The scrollback text utilities measure the visible display width of text by excluding ANSI control sequences and applying Unicode character widths.

scope.in-scope: Display width calculation with ANSI sequence exclusion, newline counting, trailing whitespace trimming, and warning-formatted string construction.
scope.boundary: Detection of CSI escape sequence boundaries is delegated to the ANSI parsing subsystem. This topic uses that detection but does not implement it.
Where and how the returned widths and formatted strings are used in display layout is handled by the scrollback buffer and layout subsystems.

behavior.display-width-calculation:
The visible display width of a text string is measured by walking the string byte by byte and applying the following logic at each position:
1. If the current position begins an ANSI CSI escape sequence, the entire sequence is skipped. No width is counted for it. Scanning resumes after the sequence ends. 2. Otherwise, the Unicode codepoint at the current position is decoded from its UTF-8 encoding. The codepoint's display width is added to the running total: - Combining characters and other zero-width codepoints contribute 0. - Narrow characters contribute 1. - Wide characters (such as CJK ideographs and many emoji) contribute 2. - Newline characters are explicitly excluded and contribute 0. 3. If a byte sequence is not valid UTF-8, the invalid portion is treated as a single character of width 1, and scanning advances by one byte.
The result is the sum of all per-codepoint widths, excluding ANSI sequences and newlines.
Terminal color and formatting codes embedded in the text do not affect the measured width. The display width reflects only the printable characters a terminal would render.
behavior.newline-counting:
The number of newline characters in a string is counted by a separate pass that applies the same ANSI-skipping logic. CSI sequences are skipped before codepoint decoding, so escape sequences that happen to contain bytes with the value of a newline are not counted as newlines.
Only the literal newline character is counted. Carriage returns and other line-ending variants are not counted.
behavior.trailing-whitespace-trimming:
Trailing whitespace is removed from a string by scanning backward from the end and removing space, tab, newline, and carriage return characters. The scan stops at the first character that is not one of these four byte values.
If the input is null or empty, an empty string is returned. The operation affects only the trailing portion of the string; leading whitespace and internal whitespace are preserved.
behavior.warning-formatting:
A warning string is constructed by prepending a warning symbol and a space to the provided text. If color output is enabled and a color code has been configured, the result is wrapped in ANSI 256-color terminal escape sequences using the configured color, with a reset sequence appended. If color output is disabled or no color code is configured, the plain symbol-prefixed string is returned without escape sequences.
The warning symbol is the Unicode warning sign character (⚠). The input text must not be null.
